accurate.why: The partial summary's claims (bill title, S. 2336, HHS grant programs, broadening beyond naloxone) are all accurate and consistent with the bill text.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated or incorrect claims appear in the summary fragment; HHS and the bill title are correctly identified.

states_purpose.why: The summary clearly identifies the bill's primary purpose of ensuring HHS opioid grant program references are not limited to naloxone.

changes_to_existing_law.why: The truncated summary never reaches the bill's amendments to existing programs (21st Century Cures Act, Public Health Service Act, State/Tribal Opioid Response Grants).

exceptions_conditions.why: The summary fails to mention the 'as appropriate' qualifier or the FDA-approval condition under section 505 limiting which reversal drugs qualify.

effective_dates.why: The summary omits the one-year deadline for HHS to update existing references.

major_provisions_covered.why: Being cut off mid-sentence, the summary misses the operative requirement to update existing references in named grant programs within one year.

objective_tone.why: The summary fragment is neutral and free of advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: The summary ends abruptly mid-sentence ('...reversal agents solely'), making it incomplete and not a finished, coherent text.

concise.why: The fragment contains no padding or filler.

correct_entities.why: HHS and the bill title/number are correctly identified in the summary fragment.

correct_figures.why: The truncated summary states no dollar amounts, dates, or quantities to evaluate.
